chapter_context.summary: Paul requests prayer for his mission and addresses the problem of idleness in the church, commanding that those who are able to work must do so, following his own example of diligence.

outline.text: Request for Prayer and Confidence in the Lord
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Request for Prayer and Confidence in the Lord (3:1-5), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Thessalonians.

outline.text: Warning Against Idleness and the Need for Work
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Warning Against Idleness and the Need for Work (3:6-15), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Thessalonians.
This section focuses on Final Benediction and Greeting (3:16-18), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 2 Thessalonians.

1.commentary:
### Tyndale Study Notes
**Chapter Overview: 2 Thessalonians 3**
This verse contributes to the central message of 2 Thessalonians, emphasizing the grace and truth revealed in Jesus Christ.
1.full_commentary:
### Matthew Henry Commentary
Matthew Henry offers devotional reflections on the spiritual impact of this passage for the believer.

### Jamieson-Fausset-Brown
JFB analysis explores the historical context and original language nuances of this portion of the New Testament.

key_themes.title: The Day of the Lord
key_themes.description: Clarifying the timing and events of Christ's return.

devotional.question: How does this passage impact your daily walk with God?

cultural_historical.title: New Testament Context
cultural_historical.text: Written in the 1st century AD to diverse groups of believers across the Roman Empire.
